title: inula glomerata
intro: Inula glomerata, also known as the ball-headed elecampane or globe fleabane, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Inula glomerata include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of Inula glomerata can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can be used to support respiratory health and overall well-being.
